name: Emily Cain, EMILY's List
subject: Your support MATTERS
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6d81/24c69fd9/161ce81fc/18918e7d/2308412705/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact")
---

Peter,

After President Trump won, women didn't sit by. They took action. They put their names on ballots. They ran for office.

And this community threw our full support behind them and helped them win elections in Virginia, Washington State, and beyond this year.

Our support over the last 12 months **mattered.** And it will be just as vital to our women from now until Election Day 2018.

We need to rally together again to raise $150,000 by the end of the month to support all of our women on the ballot next year (and three in Georgia next month!). So, I've gone through our supporter records, and this is what I have on file for this email address for the past year:
**Election Day 2017-2018 Cycle Donor Status:** Inactive
**[Suggested Action: Donate $3 to help our women win in 2018.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6d81/24c69fd9/161ce81fc/18918e7e/2308412705/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Suggested+Action+Donateif+consinConsGrou&utm_campaign=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact")**
Next year, there are nine women we need to re-elect to the Senate (our biggest group of Senate incumbents ever!), plus Jacky Rosen and Kyrsten Sinema, who can turn red seats blue.

In the House, we're 24 seats away from taking back the majority. We've already endorsed 15 women ready to flip seats with even more to come.

And winning governors' offices will be key in fighting back against the Trump administration at the state level. We've endorsed eight women who are up to the task.

Every last one of these races is crucial. Our support will help make the difference and get our women elected.

**Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal and help our women win next year:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Reach-Our-November-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6d81/24c69fd9/161ce81fc/18918e7f/2308412705/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReachOurNovember&utm_campaign=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact")**

Thanks -- and go team!

Emily Cain
Executive Director, EMILY's List

**[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6d81/24c69fd9/161ce81fc/18918e78/2308412705/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact&source=WQEFEK46N_digital_email_20171127_ElectionSupporterRecord_inact")**

name: The White House
subject: A special message for the holiday season
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a9c662c900464e9d858a1e479ebbc1bf713ba654f164b002f975840a6b7f559cdb97555cdb04147aa415bb3bf175b8f029

A special message for the holiday season

A few days ago, Americans counted our blessings as we sat down for Thanksgiving dinners with our families and friends across the country. Today, many will choose to go one step further in celebrating their good fortune by honoring #GivingTuesday, a global day of charitable giving.

This year’s #GivingTuesday is especially important. “Earlier this year, Hurricanes Harvey, Irma, and Maria devastated communities across the southern United States and in the Caribbean, and vast wildfires ravaged California,” said President Donald J. Trump in a statement. “We saw millions of Americans lose everything, from homes to businesses . . . [to] the unbearable loss of loved ones.”

The President thanks all those who have contributed to charitable organizations this year, including our houses of worship. If you would like to take part in #GivingTuesday, please consider supporting one of the great organizations partnering with us to help communities rebuild:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a9a131b28c2eb93e9c25e56fb67a9aaf34308782a65e63cb8991d423214ef41442cb8e9881eba02a96d23b27aeabc4962b
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a9be2e68d66a89ceeabd0e25e14c157cb2394896cec0f67999519e58ec49c9c511a22da619189a618a8ef708c5e43dff31

As the President said, “Together, we can ensure that the blessings of this holiday season are shared around the world.”

President Trump meets with members of the Senate Finance Committee

Yesterday, President Trump hosted Vice President Mike Pence and five members of the Senate Finance Committee at the White House for lunch in the Private Dining Room. The topic on everyone’s mind? “A tremendous tax cut – the biggest in the history of our country,” said President Trump.

The President reiterated his support for the tax bill proposed by Republican leadership and is pleased with the momentum it has inspired for passing crucial legislation well before the year’s end. As part of the Administration’s continued support, President Trump will join Senate Republicans today for lunch at the U.S. Capitol, where he’ll make the case for swift action.

Learn more about President Trump’s pro-job, pro-family, and pro-America principles for tax reform
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a90e84d957b9215b233e93007fb113a599911c1f342e068c15401de9d1522f20170c40a0223aa8e421d0ec4f45a6ab869d
here.

Bonus: Read Sen. Rand Paul’s (R-KY) op-ed on
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a9a1a113e99398de172d00b33e700d5c6a33742d36c3a077ddc089316908e01d675a1e1cb4d531bdf227d00ef14a6cc207
why he plans to vote for tax reform.

First Lady gives special thanks to Christmas volunteers

Yesterday, First Lady Melania Trump held a special gathering for volunteers who helped turn this year's White House Christmas theme, “Time-Honored Traditions,” into reality. A Gold Star Family Tree honoring service members is proudly on display in the White House East Wing, and historic pieces celebrating holiday traditions – including a nod to the first White House Christmas theme, 1961’s "Nutcracker Suite" – can be found throughout.

More than 150 volunteers came together from 29 states to assist with decorating and baking. The numbers tell the story best:

• 31,000: Number of cookies baked

• 53: Number of White House Christmas trees decorated

• 1,000: Feet of garland strung

• 12,000: Number of ornaments hung

The First Lady thanked the volunteers, who spent more than 1,600 hours bringing the First Family's first Christmas at the White House to life. She and the President look forward to hosting more than 25,000 visitors during the upcoming holiday season.

Watch First Lady Melania Trump unveil this year's Christmas decorations
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a94fd9903cd86bf3d5735ae97e7d201ca72d63d785797b241af9d8bb2e38a4b3835ea5dd26fdcd3416d0699280b8a77f4b
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump welcomes Native American Code Talkers to the White House | November 27, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Joyce N. Boghosian)

POTUS TODAY

In the early afternoon, President Trump will be at the U.S. Capitol to have lunch with Senate Republicans as they continue to work toward passing historic tax reform. Later in the day, the President will meet with congressional leadership at the White House.

Vice President Pence will travel to New York as he prepares to keynote an event celebrating the 70th anniversary of the United Nations’ vote calling for the establishment of a Jewish state in the Land of Israel. In the evening, the Vice President will deliver keynote remarks and receive the Herman Kahn Award at the Hudson Institute’s 2017 Herman Kahn Award Dinner.

COMING UP

Tomorrow, President Trump will travel to St. Charles, Missouri, to discuss the crucial need for tax reform for all Americans.

On Thursday, the First Family will participate in the National Christmas Tree Lighting on the Ellipse at the White House.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=7553867bcda662a9e1c79c8e19dd2f4d75f2a17d0029220aee0537559cad186e3d907dead68768b4c2cc555183c77e9a15c1ef56c79f3fd2
Privacy Policy |

Contact the White House |

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: I am going on the road to stop the Republican tax bill
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
This is not tax reform. It's an effort to radically transform American society and it must be defeated.
#
Sisters and Brothers -
For the past 40 years, the financial and political elite of this country have rigged the tax code to redistribute wealth and income to some of the richest and most powerful people in this country. The result: we are moving rapidly toward an oligarchic form of society in which the top 1 percent is doing phenomenally well, the middle class continues to decline and 40 million Americans are living in poverty.
And it will probably not surprise you to learn that just as our tax code benefits the wealthiest people in this country, it also benefits some of the largest and most profitable corporations in the world with a myriad of tax breaks, deductions, credits and other loopholes. As a result, one out of five large profitable corporations today pays nothing in federal taxes.
The current Republican "tax cut" bill, paid for by the Koch brothers and other billionaire campaign contributors, continues the push to make the rich richer at the expense of everyone else. It would raise taxes on middle class families making $75,000 a year or less and would throw 13 million Americans off of health insurance. And it would do all of these things to provide permanent tax cuts to the wealthiest Americans and profitable corporations that ship American jobs to China while moving their American profits to the Cayman Islands.
But let's be clear. This legislation goes well beyond taxes. Its ultimate goal is to radically transform American society and the role that government plays in the lives of the working families of our country. This legislation will increase the deficit by at least $1.5 trillion over ten years. Mark my words. If passed, the Republicans will then rediscover the "deficit crisis," and push aggressively for massive cuts in Social Security, Medicare, Medicaid, education - higher education in particular - nutrition, affordable housing and more. They will seek to undo every major piece of legislation passed in the last 80 years designed to help working families, the elderly, the children, the sick and the poor.
**This is the Republican plan. Huge tax breaks for the rich and powerful. Massive cuts to life and death programs for the middle class and working families of our country.**
**This is not moral. This is not what the American people want. This is not what our country and our pledge for "liberty and justice for all" is supposed to be about.**
**That is why I am going on the road this week to talk directly to working people in Kentucky, Ohio, and Pennsylvania about this disastrous piece of legislation. If we stand together - black, white, Latino, Asian American, Native American, male and female, young and old, gay and straight - we can defeat this horrific bill. But I need you to make your voice heard as well. We need to stand together.**
[**Please sign my petition calling on Congress to REJECT the Republican "tax cut" plan that would take from the working families in this country to give a massive tax break to people and corporations who already are doing extremely well. At a time of grotesque levels of income and wealth inequality, we must not make a bad situation even worse.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full")
Today in America, more than 40 million Americans, including 20 percent of all children, live in poverty. Many in extreme poverty. Almost 28 million Americans have no health insurance. Millions of bright kids can't afford to go to college without facing a lifetime of debt. Seniors and disabled veterans are struggling to stay alive on inadequate Social Security checks.
Despite all of that pain, the greed of the billionaire class in this country knows no limits. No. We will not allow them to take away from those in need in order to give a trillion dollars in tax breaks to the very rich.
**Here 's a radical idea for my colleagues in the Republican Party: instead of just listening to the rich and powerful few in this country, maybe just maybe Congress should listen to the majority of the American people who want a fair tax system.**
Maybe just maybe corporate tax reform should start by preventing profitable companies from sheltering profits in tax haven countries like the Cayman Islands.
Here is something you may not know:
A 2008 Government Accountability Office report found that 83 of the Fortune 100 companies use at least one offshore tax scheme to lower their taxes. A 2016 study found that one of every five large, profitable corporations paid no federal income taxes at all in 2012.
The practice of stashing profits in places like the Cayman Islands has become so absurd that one single, five-story office building there is now the official legal "home" to more than 18,000 corporations! Our tax code has essentially legalized tax dodging for large corporations.
We must stop this bill. We must stop the Republicans from moving this country into an oligarchy.
And that starts with all of us standing up, fighting back and making our voices heard. Three weeks ago progressives from coast to coast ran for office at the local and state level - and they won. We have to continue that progress and build on that momentum.
[**Please sign my petition calling on Congress to REJECT the Republican "tax cut" plan that would take from working families in this country all to give a massive tax break to people and corporations who already have it all.**](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full")
Brothers and sisters. We must do exactly the opposite of what Trump is attempting to do. He wants to divide us up by the color of our skin, our gender, our religion, our sexual orientation or our country of origin. He wants us fighting with each other while Wall Street and the billionaire class laugh all the way to the bank.
Our job is to bring our people together around an agenda that creates an economy and government that works for all, not just the 1 percent. Defeating this terrible piece of legislation will be an important step forward.
This bill is a moral abomination. I hope you'll add your name if you agree.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
****[ADD YOUR NAME](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/bernie-gop-tax?source=em171127-full")****
** **

Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123 "https://go.berniesanders.com/page/s/fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). And that starts with all of us standing up, fighting back and making our voices heard. Three weeks ago progressives from coast to coast ran for office at the local and state level - and they won. We have to continue that progress and build on that momentum. "" Brothers and sisters. We must do exactly the opposite of what Trump is attempting to do. He wants to divide us up by the color of our skin, our gender, our religion, our sexual orientation or our country of origin. He wants us fighting with each other while Wall Street and the billionaire class laugh all the way to the bank. Our job is to bring our people together around an agenda that creates an economy and government that works for all, not just the 1 percent. Defeating this terrible piece of legislation will be an important step forward. This bill is a moral abomination. I hope you'll add your name if you agree. In solidarity, Bernie Sanders ** "" ** Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders PO BOX 391, Burlington, VT 05402

body:
The GOP is after our health care once again. This time in the form of their latest tax plan. |
---

| | | Peter,

The GOP is after our health care once _again_. This time in the form of their latest tax plan.

In it, they include a provision to strip away a critical part of the Affordable Care Act, severely undermining the law that provides health care to millions of Americans.

If it were allowed to pass, premiums would increase 10% and 4 million fewer Americans would be covered in the first year. That number would rise to 13 million by 2027.

**Will you tell us how important it is to you that Joe reject the Republicans ' attempt to dismantle the health care system through their tax bill?**

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: Celebrate giving
body:
#
Hello,

Now that Black Friday and Cyber Monday are over, it's that time of the holidays where we all stop to give back -- it's #GivingTuesday!

Entering its sixth official year, #GivingTuesday has become a movement that celebrates philanthropy and works to connect individuals, communities, and organizations from diverse backgrounds for one common purpose: to celebrate and encourage giving.

**[So, on this #GivingTuesday, I'm asking you to chip in, whatever you can, to two great causes: the Hispanic Federation, and AmeriCares.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3229/9e00690/4106f04d/24033749/975062654/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-givingtuesday?utm_campaign=em17_171128_GiveTu_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171128_GiveTu_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

They are both helping lead and direct relief efforts in Puerto Rico and the U.S. Virgin Islands in the wake of this year's tragic hurricanes.

The Hispanic Federation is a Latino nonprofit membership organization that seeks to support Hispanic families and strengthen Latino institutions through work in areas like education, health, immigration, and civic engagement. A coalition of elected officials in New York and Puerto Rico joined the Hispanic Federation to step up and help the millions of American citizens that suffered -- even when our own federal government failed to intervene quickly enough. They launched the United for Puerto Rico Relief Fund for those affected by Maria, and proceeds will go to community and civic organizations on the island.

AmeriCares is an independent relief and development organization, which provides health services to those in need and is working with officials in the U.S. Virgin Islands and Puerto Rico to stock emergency shelters with medical supplies. Their emergency programs help communities prepare for, respond to and recover from disasters and provide millions of people around the world access to medicine and supplies.

**[Chip in today and donate to AmeriCares and the Hispanic Federation this #GivingTuesday.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3229/9e00690/4106f04d/24033749/975062654/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-givingtuesday?utm_campaign=em17_171128_GiveTu_fnd_gq_ful__AG&utm_medium=email&utm_source=Gillum&refcode=em17_171128_GiveTu_fnd_gq_ful__AG")**

Our brothers and sisters are suffering and need our help this holiday season.

Thank you,

Andrew

name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: we can't give up
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Van Hollen for US Senate
body:
| __ We're at a pivotal moment in American politics.

Republicans are spreading a false narrative that their tax plan will actually help working people and that no one in the middle class will see a tax increase. That's dead wrong, and the American people deserve to know the truth.
Instead of standing up for what's right, Republicans are lining up to provide big corporations and the very wealthy with a massive tax break and leave the rest of the country to pick up the tab.

That's why I'm on my feet, fighting back against the crushing Republican plan. And it's why we need a Democratic majority in the Senate.

[**Will you stand with me to help elect strong Democrats to the Senate in 2018? We must raise $10,000 by midnight Thursday >>>**](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b87/70cdc2ba/46cad5c7/3902de9/329823500/VEsH/ "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/eom11?refcode=em112817&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=1+-+Will+you+stand+with+me+to+help+elect+str&utm_campaign=em112817&source=em112817")

Meanwhile, two weeks from today Alabama will hold an election where the choice is crystal clear. On the one hand, we have Doug Jones, a former U.S. Attorney who successfully prosecuted members of the KKK for the bombing of the 16th Street Baptist Church in Birmingham that killed four little girls. On the other hand, there is Roy Moore.

Simply put, Ivanka Trump had it right when she said "there is a special place in hell for those that prey on children." Unfortunately her father is supporting the child abuser.

[**I am doing everything I can to help Doug Jones in this special election, but I need your help. Please help me send a strong message to Donald Trump that Roy Moore is unfit to serve in the U.S. Senate. I need your help now. **](http://action.vanhollen.org/page/m/2de91b87/70cdc2ba/46cad5c7/3902de6/329823500/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/eom11?refcode=em112817&amount=25.00&recurring=auto&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cvanhollen&utm_content=2+-+I+am+doing+everything+I+can+to+help+Doug&utm_campaign=em112817&source=em112817")

Thanks for your support at this important moment, Chris Van Hollen
U.S. Senator

name: Lucinda Guinn, EMILY's List
subject: 19 women who can help flip the House
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e6b/24c69fd9/160d63c89/1891bf25/4214678510/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact")
---

Peter,

We could flip the House in 2018.

A couple months ago, we started thinking it was a possibility. With the results of the 2017 elections (so far), things are definitely trending our way.

But we also know nothing is an inevitability in elections, and a lot can happen in a year.

Which is why we spent the last year recruiting top-tier candidates, and now we're helping them build strong campaigns that can handle whatever comes their way.

**[Donate $3 to help reach our End-of-Month goal of $150,000 so we can continue to support women who can flip the House.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e6b/24c69fd9/160d63c89/1891bf26/4214678510/VEsE/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=2+-+Donate+3+to+help+reach+our+EndofMonth+go&utm_campaign=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact")**

Beyond the 2017 election results, there are two major polling numbers that show good signs for us: the generic ballot and President Trump's approval rating.

Since Election Day 2017, _FiveThirtyEight's_ daily average shows Democrats leading by nine to 11 points on a generic ballot. And _FiveThirtyEight's_ average of Trump's approval rating hasn't gone above 40 percent since May.

But building a wave that can sweep Democrats into the House isn't just about math. It's also about finding top-tier candidates (which we've done) and having their backs through competitive primaries and then tough general elections against Republicans -- who've shown us this year that there is literally no low they won't sink to.

We have already endorsed 19 House women who have what it takes to win. And we will endorse more before 2017 is over.

**Donate $3 to help reach our $150,000 End-of-Month goal and take back the House:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Reach-Our-November-Goal](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e6b/24c69fd9/160d63c89/1891bf27/4214678510/VEsF/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=3+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgReachOurNovember&utm_campaign=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact")**

Thanks,

Lucinda Guinn
Vice President of Campaigns, EMILY's List

[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6e6b/24c69fd9/160d63c89/1891bf20/4214678510/VEsC/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171122_email_EOMNovember?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=4&utm_campaign=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact&source=WQEFEK48N_digital_email_20171128_EOMFlipTheHouse_inact")

name: David Driscoll, The Sanders Institute
subject: GOP Tax Plan - Who Benefits?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
#
Friend,
"_Don 't fall for supply-side, trickle-down nonsense. Lower taxes on the rich don't generate growth and jobs. They only make the rich even richer, at a time of raging inequality, and they cause bigger budget deficits._" - Robert Reich, Sanders Institute Founding Fellow
Congress is now considering a proposed GOP tax plan and is expected to vote on it in the coming weeks. Who benefits? Who doesn't? We all need to understand the impact of the proposed bill and let our voices be heard before the vote takes place. We at the Sanders Institute have compiled some information from our fellows and other resources to give you some answers:
[_**Why The Republican Tax Plan Is More Failed Trickle-Down Economics**_](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ae03/bed43eb/29540be2/75ff64f2/228133082/VEsH/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/why-the-republican-tax-plan-is-more-failed-trickle-down-economics")
This article from Robert Reich succinctly explains why Trickle-Down Economics just does not work. He gives clear, historical examples of when trickle-down economics has been implemented and failed and when the opposite, tax increases on the wealthy, have worked.
[_**CBO Projects the Impact of GOP Tax Plan**_](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ae03/bed43eb/29540be2/75ff64fd/228133082/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/congressional-budget-office-cost-estimate-the-tax-cuts-and-jobs-act")
The nonpartisan Congressional Budget Office released this report on Sunday. It clearly shows that the federal government would be raising taxes on those making less and generously benefiting those making more. For instance, Americans making less than $30,000 in 2019 will pay $2,580,000,000 **more** in taxes - while those making over $200,000 will pay $118,550,000,000 **less** in taxes in 2019.

[_**The GOP Wants To Eliminate The Estate Tax**_](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7ae03/bed43eb/29540be2/75ff64fc/228133082/VEsF/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/the-gop-wants-to-eliminate-the-estate-tax-lets-use-it-to-expand-social-security-instead")
This article outlines a bold new idea when talking about the Estate Tax, a tax that the GOP plan would repeal. It argues that the growing inequality in the United States has negatively affected Social Security and suggests that we keep the Estate Tax to reduce that inequality and make Social Security more sustainable.
There is a lot at stake in this far-reaching legislation. Educate yourselves, take a stand, and let your voices be heard.
Thank you for staying engaged,
David Driscoll
Executive Director
The Sanders Institute

name: Jay Inslee
subject: Our climate leadership - North to South
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We're in it together as we take on climate change.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTI0NTcwMjYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Friend, Climate change is a global issue, but the consequences are local. You and I both know: From historic wildfires to devastating floods, we've seen that local impact in terrible ways throughout 2017. That's why I traveled to British Columbia to speak to their Legislative Assembly last week. I was the first Washington governor to do so in many years. I took that trip because extraordinary times call for extraordinary action. **Climate change is too important to me, to Washington, and to this country, to sit back.** I'm committed to leading. And national leadership on climate will start with the Great Blue Wall along the Pacific -- three states led by a progressive majority -- with the help of our neighbors to the north, fighting to counteract Trump's inaction on climate change. [**Help me build up this bank of progressive power and chip in $3 towards our $25k official end-of-month deadline.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=11&ms=MTI0NTcwMjYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&mt=2&rj=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&rt=0) [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTI0NTcwMjYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0) Our ability to battle climate change is what we will be remembered for a century from now. So while President Trump would relinquish America's leadership, I'm stepping up to the plate. We need local and international leadership to turn the tide of climate change. In British Columbia last week, Premier John Horgan and I resolved to strengthen our partnership and act jointly to fight climate change across the region. We agree that now, more than ever, we must take bold action to demonstrate climate leadership. I'm ready to stand up for America as a leader. But to do it, I need your support. I set a $25,000 end-of-month deadline to prove that we're in it together as we take on climate change. [**Chip in $3 right now towards our official goal and show the strength of our climate movement.**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=8&ms=MTI0NTcwMjYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&mt=2&rj=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&rt=0) Very truly yours, Jay Inslee | [CONTRIBUTE »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=5&ms=MTI0NTcwMjYS1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTI4MjYwMzg4NQS2&mt=1&rt=0)
